title: Thread for the Proposal of the EMPP
display_title: Thread for the Proposal of the EMPP
wikitext:
[]: Thread for the Proposal of the EMPP Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Sat Nov 27, 2010 11:23 pm EMPP stands for the Evangelion Media Propogation Project. I want to propose this idea as a voluntary function of evageeks. By doing this we can make eva media that is out of print more availible to individuals who desire it but do not want to take actions fringing on illegal such as torrenting. These would be the main things we would want to start with: Providing AVI format copies of the Evangelion TV Series. Providing AVI format copies of EoE and Death/Rebirth. Converting Angelic Days manga to JPEG format. Any other suggestions can be brought forward at a later time, this is just a starting base composed of eva media we lost with the collapse of ADV. All I need is the support of a few dedicated and resourceful members and the permission of the board staff. I plan to accuire all of the above things through strictly legal channels.

[]: Post by esselfortium » Sat Nov 27, 2010 11:31 pm Er... how exactly do you plan on legally "providing" AVIs of Evangelion without owning any of the rights to it?

[]: Post by Sachi » Sat Nov 27, 2010 11:37 pm esselfortium wrote: Er... how exactly do you plan on legally "providing" AVIs of Evangelion without owning any of the rights to it?

[]: Post by Baz » Sat Nov 27, 2010 11:39 pm Also, the TV series is not out of print. ADV still exists, still holds the license, and someone (either ADV or their creditor) gets money from every copy that is sold. Unless I'm mistaken...

[]: Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Sat Nov 27, 2010 11:41 pm esselfortium wrote: Er... how exactly do you plan on legally "providing" AVIs of Evangelion without owning any of the rights to it?

[]: Post by Sachi » Sat Nov 27, 2010 11:44 pm supershinjiasukashipper wrote: Simply put, nobody has the rights to it so there is nobody to complain, and when somebody gets the rights we simply remove the media since there is no need for us to provide it anymore at that time.

[]: Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Sat Nov 27, 2010 11:48 pm Sachi wrote: So, you plan to distribute Eva and related material over the internet without any rights of distribution? Yeah, I see this getting somewhere.

[]: Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Nov 27, 2010 11:57 pm Okay. I understand the point of this. But let me assure you. There is no point to this. Evangelion is out of print, but still decently available. Sites like eBay make things relatively easy to get a hold of. The TV series is still licensed by Section 23 or whatever, and I would assume they might also still have the rights to "Angelic Days". Regardless, you can still find both of those on Amazon and eBay. It is available. The movies, yes, they are both out of print, and unlicensed, but can be had from Amazon and eBay. I would like to see Evangelion remain in print. And I do hope that FUNimation picks up the rights to The End of Evangelion, at the very least. Doggy, if you're serious about wanting Evangelion's availability to be preserved, email FUNimation, encourage them to get The End of Evangelion. We need a new, better DVD of that more than anything else. Also, you can write to Viz, and ask they reprint volume 9 of the manga. That shit is hard to find.

[]: Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Sun Nov 28, 2010 12:04 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Okay. I understand the point of this. But let me assure you. There is no point to this. Evangelion is out of print, but still decently available. Sites like eBay make things relatively easy to get a hold of. The TV series is still licensed by Section 23 or whatever, and I would assume they might also still have the rights to "Angelic Days". Regardless, you can still find both of those on Amazon and eBay. It is available. The movies, yes, they are both out of print, and unlicensed, but can be had from Amazon and eBay. I would like to see Evangelion remain in print. And I do hope that FUNimation picks up the rights to The End of Evangelion, at the very least. Doggy, if you're serious about wanting Evangelion's availability to be preserved, email FUNimation, encourage them to get The End of Evangelion. We need a new, better DVD of that more than anything else. Also, you can write to Viz, and ask they reprint volume 9 of the manga. That shit is hard to find.

[]: Post by esselfortium » Sun Nov 28, 2010 12:05 am supershinjiasukashipper wrote: Simply put, nobody has the rights to it so there is nobody to complain, and when somebody gets the rights we simply remove the media since there is no need for us to provide it anymore at that time.

[]: Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Nov 28, 2010 12:09 am supershinjiasukashipper wrote: Than lets make a point of that, even better clark-sama, you print out a list of members on this website and mail it to them so they can see the market there is for it.

[]: Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Sun Nov 28, 2010 12:15 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Than lets make a point of that, even better clark-sama, you print out a list of members on this website and mail it to them so they can see the market there is for it. A petition? Those don't really work. Companies like to see people going out of their way to say "THIS IS WHAT I WANT" in the most direct way possible. If you want a company to do something, you have to show them something real and tangible. I don't have a credit card. I have a debit card. Online sales (like eBay) open up wondrous doors to collectors. Just be smart about it.

[]: Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Nov 28, 2010 12:22 am supershinjiasukashipper wrote: Well its not a petition, its more like showing the potential market to these companies in the hopes that they will invest in our intrests.

[]: Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Sun Nov 28, 2010 12:29 am The Eva Monkey wrote: I don't understand. Are you saying we should be like "Hey anime company, we have a big Evangelion website, so license Evangelion stuff"? Your money is never safe. From debit scanners on ATM machines, to malwares that log your online banking, your money is always at risk. The key is to only use trustworthy systems, like PayPal for example, which is what is used to make eBay purchases. Amazon is also a trust worthy seller.

[]: Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Nov 28, 2010 12:36 am supershinjiasukashipper wrote: Something like that, we say we have a big eva website with more people joining everyday, and thus the market for eva is not only large but still expanding.

[]: Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Sun Nov 28, 2010 12:42 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Sorry dude, but that's not how it works. They're not idiots. They know fansites exist. What they need to know is that there are tangible, real leads out there. They need people to show them what they want, and that they're willing to spend real money on a product. If enough people do that, at a steady pace, over time, they start to think "People really want this" and they pursue licenses. In the case of Funi, they are/were going after EoE. So right now is a great time to make direct communications to their acquisitions people that they should pick it up, because it will be worth it, because there are people who want an updated version. Like, if it were me, I would show them that I have 3 iterations of EoE and D&R, because I'm a collector, and I will buy any new version of it. And they need casual fans saying, I have the old version, but I really want a new version. Be direct. Be tangible. Write to them using your real name. Send them a video on YouTube. Write them an honest to goodness REAL TANGIBLE LETTER. Go the extra mile.

[]: Post by Sachi » Sun Nov 28, 2010 12:43 am supershinjiasukashipper wrote: My idea of the extra mile is arranging an appointment with one of their reps and making a presentation demonstrating the current market and projecting future sales.

[]: Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Sun Nov 28, 2010 12:49 am Sachi wrote: Then, by all means, go for that.

[]: Post by Baz » Sun Nov 28, 2010 12:52 am Dude... fight your own battles.

[]: Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Sun Nov 28, 2010 12:54 am Baz wrote: Dude... fight your own battles.
